subject: Databases
title: Designing for Write-Heavy Workloads
description: A micro-lesson that makes Designing for Write-Heavy Workloads usable.

hook.text: An IoT platform ingests a million events per second. Too many indexes and the writes can't keep up.

buildup.text: Minimize indexes on write-heavy tables. Every index must be updated on every INSERT.

discovery.text: Append-only tables work well: no updates, just inserts. Time-series and event logs fit this pattern.

twist.text: Partitioning splits one table into many. Writes spread across partitions. Old partitions can be dropped cheaply.

climax.text: Buffer writes in memory (like Redis) and flush to the database in batches. Reduces per-row overhead.

punchline.text: Write-heavy? Fewer indexes, append-only, batch inserts.

quiz.question: Why should you minimize indexes on write-heavy tables?
options: Each index must be updated on every INSERT, slowing down write throughput
Indexes don't work on tables with many rows
Write-heavy tables can't have any indexes at all
